title: rubus × tenuicuspis
intro: Rubus × tenuicuspis, also known as Himalayan blackberry, is a hybrid species of bramble that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × tenuicuspis include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Rubus × tenuicuspis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused in oil, or taken as a supplement, with its leaves and berries being the most commonly used parts of the plant.
